title: Reduce MoonMind Docker image build time by removing duplicate frontend build work and improving Docker cache reuse
description:
## Jira issue: Reduce MoonMind Docker image build time by removing duplicate frontend build work and improving Docker cache reuse
### Summary
MoonMind Docker image builds currently take roughly 6–11 minutes. Implement the first three Docker build-speed improvements identified during review:
1. Remove duplicate frontend build work from the Docker publish workflow while keeping Docker as the source of truth for final frontend assets.
2. Reorder the runtime project install so pip install --no-deps . is invalidated by fewer non-package file changes.
3. Add BuildKit cache mounts for npm, pip, and apt package operations.
For the frontend build de-duplication, choose the option with the least artifact-pipeline customization: do not introduce upload/download artifact plumbing for frontend assets. Instead, keep the Dockerfile’s frontend-builder stage responsible for producing the assets that ship in the image, and adjust the GitHub Actions pre-build checks so they do not run the full Vite production build a second time.
### Background
The Dockerfile already separates major build concerns into tooling-builder, frontend-builder, python-requirements, and runtime stages. The frontend stage installs npm dependencies from package.json / package-lock.json, copies the frontend inputs, runs npm run ui:build, and verifies the generated manifest.
The publish workflow also runs frontend CI before the Docker image build. In the current repo workflow, the amd64 job runs npm ci, then npm run frontend:ci, then npm run contracts:check; frontend:ci includes typecheck, lint, tests, and ui:build:check, which itself performs a Vite build and manifest verification.
The Dockerfile then performs the frontend production build again inside frontend-builder, and because the Docker publish workflow builds per platform, this Dockerfile stage may run as part of both platform builds.
The runtime stage also copies multiple runtime asset trees before running pip install --no-deps ., meaning changes to api_service, config, release notes, .agents, or moonspec can invalidate the Python project install layer even though pyproject.toml only declares moonmind as the installed Python package.
Finally, the Dockerfile already opts into Dockerfile syntax 1.4, which supports BuildKit cache mounts, but the current npm, pip, and apt package operations do not use cache mounts.
### Goals
Reduce warm and incremental Docker build time without changing the contents or behavior of the published api-runtime image.
Preserve existing validation coverage where practical: frontend typecheck, lint, tests, generated contract checks, Docker-contained frontend asset build, manifest verification, CLI verification, and Python package installation should still run.
Avoid adding new frontend artifact upload/download/storage conventions to the GitHub Actions pipeline.
### Non-goals
Do not introduce a separate prebuilt runtime base image in this ticket.
Do not change the production image target, published tags, manifest creation, or GHCR publishing flow.
Do not pin CLI/base image versions as part of this issue unless needed to unblock the cache-mount changes.
Do not split main/test Poetry requirements export in this issue; that is a separate proposal.
## Implementation plan
### 1. Remove duplicate frontend production build from Docker publish workflow
Use the low-customization option: keep the Dockerfile as the single source of truth for production frontend assets.
Update .github/workflows/docker-publish.yml so the pre-Docker amd64 checks no longer run npm run frontend:ci if that script still includes ui:build:check.
Recommended workflow change:
- name: Check frontend and generated contracts
  if: matrix.platform == 'linux/amd64'
  run: |
    npm ci
    npm run ui:typecheck
    npm run ui:lint
    npm run ui:test
    npm run contracts:check
This preserves frontend typecheck, lint, unit tests, and generated contract checks, while avoiding the extra npm run ui:build:check production build in the workflow.
Leave the Dockerfile’s frontend-builder stage unchanged in responsibility: it should continue to run the production Vite build and tools/verify_vite_manifest.py before assets are copied into the runtime image.
Optional cleanup: add a package script such as:
"frontend:ci:no-build": "npm run ui:typecheck && npm run ui:lint && npm run ui:test"
Then the workflow step can call:
npm run frontend:ci:no-build
npm run contracts:check
Prefer the inline workflow commands if minimizing package script churn is more important than reuse.
### 2. Reorder project install to reduce invalidation of pip install --no-deps .
Currently, runtime asset directories are copied before the editable/project install equivalent:
COPY moonmind /app/moonmind/
COPY api_service /app/api_service/
COPY config /app/config/
COPY docs/ReleaseNotes /app/docs/ReleaseNotes/
COPY .agents /app/.agents/
COPY moonspec /app/moonspec/
...
RUN pip install --disable-pip-version-check --no-deps .
Change this so the Python package install depends only on packaging metadata and the actual Python package directory:
COPY pyproject.toml /app/pyproject.toml
COPY poetry.lock /app/poetry.lock
COPY README.md /app/README.md

RUN pip install --disable-pip-version-check readmeai~=0.6.3 --no-deps

COPY moonmind /app/moonmind/
RUN pip install --disable-pip-version-check --no-deps .

# Copy non-package runtime assets after the project install.
COPY api_service /app/api_service/
COPY config /app/config/
COPY docs/ReleaseNotes /app/docs/ReleaseNotes/
COPY .agents /app/.agents/
COPY moonspec /app/moonspec/
Keep the MoonSpec projection checks after .agents and moonspec are copied:
RUN test -f /app/moonspec/bundle/moonspec.bundle.yaml \
    && test -f /app/moonspec/bundle/skills/moonspec-verify/SKILL.md \
    && test -f /app/.agents/skills/moonspec-verify/SKILL.md
Keep the frontend asset copies after the runtime app files are in place:
COPY --from=frontend-builder /build/api_service/static/workflow_console/ /app/api_service/static/workflow_console/
COPY --from=frontend-builder /build/api_service/static/workflow_console/ /opt/moonmind_static/workflow_console/
This should make edits to templates, config, release notes, .agents, or MoonSpec files less likely to invalidate the Python package install layer.
### 3. Add BuildKit cache mounts for package-manager-heavy steps
Use RUN --mount=type=cache for npm, pip, and apt operations. The Dockerfile already starts with # syntax=docker/dockerfile:1.4, so no syntax upgrade should be required.
#### npm cache mounts
Apply npm cache mounts to:
RUN /bin/bash /tmp/install_cli_tooling.sh && rm -f /tmp/install_cli_tooling.sh
and:
RUN npm ci --no-fund --no-audit
Example:
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm \
    /bin/bash /tmp/install_cli_tooling.sh \
    && rm -f /tmp/install_cli_tooling.sh
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm \
    npm ci --no-fund --no-audit
Update api_service/docker/install_cli_tooling.sh so it does not erase the BuildKit npm cache. Remove or gate the current npm cache clean --force behavior when using Docker cache mounts.
Suggested low-risk approach:
if [ "${MOONMIND_CLEAN_NPM_CACHE:-false}" = "true" ]; then
    npm cache clean --force || true
fi
Then leave MOONMIND_CLEAN_NPM_CACHE unset in Docker builds.
#### pip cache mounts
Apply pip cache mounts to Poetry installation, main requirements installation, readmeai installation, project install, and test requirements installation.
Examples:
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/pip \
    pip install --disable-pip-version-check \
        poetry==2.4.1 \
        poetry-plugin-export==1.10.0
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/pip \
    pip install --disable-pip-version-check -r /tmp/requirements-main.txt \
    && rm -f /tmp/requirements-main.txt
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/pip \
    pip install --disable-pip-version-check readmeai~=0.6.3 --no-deps
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/pip \
    pip install --disable-pip-version-check --no-deps .
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/pip \
    if [ "${INSTALL_TEST_DEPS}" = "true" ]; then \
        pip install --disable-pip-version-check -r /tmp/requirements-tests.txt; \
    fi && \
    rm -f /tmp/requirements-tests.txt
#### apt cache mounts
Before apt cache mounts are useful, disable Docker’s default apt cache cleanup and tell apt to keep downloaded packages.
Add this once per Debian-based stage that uses apt heavily, or factor it into each relevant stage before apt installs:
RUN rm -f /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/docker-clean \
    && echo 'Binary::apt::APT::Keep-Downloaded-Packages "true";' \
       >/etc/apt/apt.conf.d/keep-cache
Then update apt install steps to use locked cache mounts:
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/var/cache/apt,sharing=locked \
    --mount=type=cache,target=/var/lib/apt,sharing=locked \
    apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        python3 \
        build-essential && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
Apply the same pattern to:
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3 && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
and the larger runtime apt block that installs bubblewrap, ripgrep, tesseract-ocr, git, gh, Docker CLI, Compose plugin, and related packages.
Keep the final rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* if image size is a concern; the cache mount should still preserve downloaded packages outside the image layer.
## Acceptance criteria
The Docker publish workflow no longer runs npm run frontend:ci if that script still includes the production Vite build.
The Docker publish workflow still runs frontend typecheck, lint, UI tests, and generated contract checks before building/pushing images.
The Dockerfile still builds frontend production assets inside the frontend-builder stage and still runs tools/verify_vite_manifest.py.
No new frontend build artifact upload/download step is introduced.
pip install --disable-pip-version-check --no-deps . runs immediately after copying moonmind/ and before copying broad non-package runtime asset directories such as api_service, config, docs/ReleaseNotes, .agents, and moonspec.
MoonSpec projection checks still run after .agents and moonspec are copied.
npm install steps use BuildKit cache mounts.
pip install steps use BuildKit cache mounts.
apt install steps use BuildKit cache mounts with locked sharing.
install_cli_tooling.sh no longer clears the npm cache during normal Docker builds, or cache clearing is gated behind an opt-in environment variable.
A clean Docker build of target api-runtime succeeds.
A repeated Docker build of target api-runtime shows improved cache reuse in --progress=plain output for npm, pip, and apt-related steps.
The published runtime image still starts with /app/api_service/entrypoint.sh, exposes port 8000, includes the workflow console static assets in both expected locations, and passes the existing CLI verification step for codex, claude, npm, and rg.
### Suggested validation commands
docker buildx build \
  --progress=plain \
  --file ./api_service/Dockerfile \
  --target api-runtime \
  --build-arg INSTALL_CODEX_CLI=true \
  --build-arg INSTALL_CLAUDE_CLI=true \
  .
Run the same command twice and compare the second build’s output for cache reuse.
Also run the publish workflow on a branch and confirm:
Check frontend and generated contracts
Build and push by digest
Create manifest list and push
all complete successfully for the expected matrix platforms.
### Risks / notes
Removing the workflow-level ui:build:check means the production frontend build is validated only inside the Docker build path for this workflow. This is intentional for this issue to avoid duplicate build work.
Do not switch to “build frontend once, upload artifact, download artifact into both platform builds” in this ticket. That may save more work later, but it requires additional artifact handling and .dockerignore consideration.
Apt cache mounts may require small adjustments if package index cleanup prevents effective reuse. Prioritize correctness and image size first, then tune cache persistence.
The npm cache cleanup in install_cli_tooling.sh directly conflicts with persistent BuildKit npm cache reuse, so it must be removed or made opt-in for this issue to deliver the intended speedup.
